items.title: Do Carney and Poilievre have different visions for the Canada-U.S. relationship?
items.summary_auto: The clearest attempt at a line of demarcation during Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre's speech on Canada-United States relations was an apparent rejoinder to Prime Minister Mark Carney's insistence that a "rupture" has occurred.

items.title: Canadians are drinking less, driving the biggest drop in alcohol sales in 20 years: StatsCan
items.summary_auto: Canadians are buying less alcohol, leading to the largest annual drop in beer, wine, spirits and cider sales in the 20 years Statistics Canada has been tracking the data, according to the agency's new report.

items.title: Canada's auto market is officially open to Chinese EVs, but you won't see cheaper models right away
items.summary_auto: The Canadian auto market officially opened to Chinese-made electric vehicles on March 1. But that doesn't mean you'll be able to drive a BYD or a Chery EV on Canadian streets anytime soon.

items.title: With Trump visit coming up, China hedges its bets on helping Iran
items.summary_auto: China’s latest blueprint for driving growth in its economy was largely written before the U.S. and Israel instigated the most far-reaching war in the Middle East in decades. But when the Chinese premier stood up in Beijing’s Great Hall of the People on Thursday to deliver the opening address, some last-minute updates appeared evident.

items.title: Meet the microdramas drawing in viewers — and creating entertainment jobs in Canada
items.summary_auto: Packed with revenge tropes and high-drama plots, microdramas are served up in one- to two-minute chunks in a vertical aspect ratio, straight to your smartphone. Producers say it's the “future” of the entertainment industry, but fans and unions still have issues with the quality, and how performers will be protected.

items.title: What airlines are supposed to do when uncontrollable circumstances lead to flight disruptions
items.summary_auto: Canadians preparing to catch a flight this month should familiarize themselves with the Air Passenger Protection Regulations (APPR), so they know what airlines should do if their flights be cancelled or delayed amid uncontrollable circumstances.

items.title: Astronomers are capturing video of a black hole for the first time
items.summary_auto: Astronomers are working to capture the first-ever video of a black hole — a groundbreaking effort that could shed light on how these enigmatic cosmic objects behave and offer clues about the origins of our universe.

items.title: Hezbollah joins Iran's fight even as other allies melt into Axis of Reluctance
items.summary_auto: For decades, Iran has protected itself with a network of well-funded, heavily armed and deeply radicalized militant groups. But just when they are needed most, many of the main players are staying on the sidelines, with only Hezbollah, Iran’s first proxy, helping out.

items.title: U.S. says it worked with Ecuador to bomb alleged drug-trafficking operations site
items.summary_auto: The U.S. and Ecuador carried out a joint operation targeting drug trafficking operations in the ‌South American country, authorities in both countries said on Friday, with the U.S. calling the move "lethal kinetic operations."

items.title: Canadians feel the pinch at the pump as conflict in the Middle East drives up gas prices
items.summary_auto: Canadians saw fuel prices climbing across the country this week as the conflict in the Middle East drives up the price of oil. One expert says those high numbers are likely to stick around for a while, even if the war ends in the next few weeks.

items.title: Slain Windsor woman expressed fears to CBC News days before she died
items.summary_auto: Less than a week before she died, a woman stabbed in a small southwestern Ontario town had spoken to CBC about the threats she was facing for speaking out about Khalistan extremism.

items.title: Flag raising fosters connection between Ghanaians in Prince George
items.summary_auto: A flag raising to mark Ghana Independence Day at Prince George's city hall brought members of the Ghanaian community together. Now, the Prince George Ghana Association says it wants to create its own community centre.

items.title: Carney says Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor should be removed from line of succession
Trump to end boycott of White House Correspondents' Dinner | About That
items.summary_auto: Andrew Chang explains why U.S. President Donald Trump decided to attend this year's White House Correspondents' Dinner for the first time as a president, after boycotting the annual event during his first term. Images provided by The Canadian Press, Reuters and Getty Images

items.title: Judge indicates Frank Stronach to be found not guilty on at least 3 charges, as trial continues
items.summary_auto: Frank Stronach will no longer face an attempted rape charge connected to one of the complainants in his sexual assault trial, court heard on Friday. Ontario Superior Court Justice Anne Molloy indicated she will be entering a not guilty verdict on that count, along with two other counts, meaning the Canadian businessman now faces nine charges instead of the original 12.

items.title: Man charged with arranging shootings in Toronto police corruption probe denied bail
items.summary_auto: A Richmond Hill, Ont., man who is accused of arranging seven shootings connected with a major police corruption and organized crime investigation was denied bail Friday in Oshawa, Ont.

items.title: Daryl Hannah denounces portrayal in JFK Jr.-Carolyn Bessette series Love Story
items.summary_auto: Daryl Hannah has issued a scathing response to the new TV series about John F. Kennedy Jr. and wife Carolyn Bessette, denouncing the way she's been portrayed in the FX and Hulu show as false, damaging and misogynistic.

items.title: Montreal teacher challenges school policy on hiding student gender identity from parents
items.summary_auto: Quebec's Superior Court heard arguments Friday in a case about a high school teacher who alleges her Charter rights were violated when she was ordered to hide a student's gender identity from their parents.

items.title: Heated Rivalry show creator Jacob Tierney's next project is another CanLit adaptation
items.summary_auto: Heated Rivalry showrunner Jacob Tierney will write, direct and executive produce new series Alexander, adapted from Vancouver author Annabel Lyon's 2009 novel The Golden Mean, Netflix announced Thursday.
